title: Dog Tremors After Eating Compost: Urgent Safety Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: If your dog has tremors after eating compost, they may have mycotoxin poisoning. This is a high-priority emergency. Learn what to do immediately.
content_html:
What Is Tremorgenic Mycotoxin Poisoning?

When dogs consume decomposing organic matter like compost, moldy food, or yard waste, they often ingest fungi that produce tremorgenic mycotoxins. These are potent neurotoxins that directly affect a dog's central nervous system, leading to involuntary muscle tremors, lack of coordination, and potentially life-threatening seizures.

Is It an Emergency? (Urgency Level: High)

Yes, this is a critical veterinary emergency. Symptoms can progress rapidly from mild twitching to full-body seizures and severe hyperthermia (overheating) due to constant muscle activity. There is no at-home cure for mycotoxin poisoning; your dog requires immediate professional intervention to stabilize their tremors, protect their organ function, and prevent permanent neurological damage.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If it is safe to do so, taking a quick photo of the compost pile or any moldy material your dog ingested can be incredibly helpful for the veterinary team. A visual record allows the vet to identify the potential source of the toxin and assess the volume of material consumed. Additionally, if your dog has already vomited, a photo of the contents can provide clues about how recently the ingestion occurred and what other hazards might be present.

faqs.question: How soon do symptoms start after eating compost?
faqs.answer: Symptoms typically appear within 30 minutes to three hours after ingestion, though they can sometimes manifest sooner depending on the amount consumed.

faqs.question: What are the common signs of mycotoxin poisoning?
faqs.answer: Common signs include fine muscle tremors (looking like shivering), drooling, vomiting, a staggered walk (ataxia), and increased heart rate.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: No. You should never induce vomiting without direct instructions from a veterinarian, especially if your dog is already showing neurological signs like tremors, as this can lead to aspiration pneumonia.

faqs.question: What is the prognosis for dogs who eat compost?
faqs.answer: With rapid veterinary treatment including decontamination, IV fluids, and anti-seizure medication, the prognosis is generally good, but delays in care can be fatal.
